_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: FL state-leg batch: HD-23 Grow, SD-24 Bernard, HD-24 Chamberlin, SD-25 Arrington, HD-25 Yarkosky. Evidence-first; cited TRUE/FALSE, null where no evidence.

profile.background: Freshman R, FL HD-23 (Citrus + SW Marion). First elected Nov 2024 (74% over Dem Judith Vowels), sworn Nov 19 2024, succeeding term-limited Ralph Massullo. Agribusiness owner. Thin freshman record — most marquee FL bills predate his tenure.

jj-grow@FL.notes_set: Freshman (Nov 2024), short documented record. Pro-life Christian, fiscal-conservative, pro-police self-presentation. Co-sponsored HB751 LEO benefits (2025). Voted YEA on HB1205 (2025, initiative-petition crackdown) and HB1255 (2025 education). No published FRTL/NRA/JMI rating yet. Immigration special-session roll calls not individually confirmable. No cross-party deviations found; record directionally conservative but evidence-light.

0.note: Self-described 'pro-life Christian' in campaign coverage (SOFT, own stated position).
Campaigned on 'support for law enforcement'; co-sponsored CS/HB751 (2025) LEO/correctional officer benefits — affirmative pro-police advocacy.

profile.background: Sitting FL Senator SD-24 (D, coastal Palm Beach County). Seated Nov 5 2024 via unopposed seat-swap with Bobby Powell. Haitian-born; prior FL House (2009-12) and Palm Beach County Commissioner (2016-24, Mayor 2018-19). Office classification CONFIRMED correct (state senator, not the prior county-commission role).

mack-bernard@FL.notes_set: Office verified correct — sitting state senator, not county commissioner (that was his prior office). Freshman senator; legislative footprint concentrated on housing, homestead/property-tax relief, insurance, criminal justice. Vice Chair, Ethics & Elections. Hard cited values vote: NAY on CS/SB 2B (2025 Special Session B, 'TRUMP Act' immigration-enforcement, passed 21-16) — standard Democratic position. No conservative deviations found; no individually-cited liberal votes on life/marriage/guns (NO EVIDENCE FOUND, left null).

2.note: Voted NAY on CS/SB 2B (2025) 'TRUMP Act' mandating state cooperation with federal immigration enforcement — documented opposition to enforcement/anti-sanctuary measures.

profile.background: R, FL HD-24 (Marion/Ocala). Won 2023 special election (succeeding indicted Joe Harding), re-elected Nov 2024; term to Nov 2026. CEO of True Patriot Network; Trump-aligned. Self-described 'red-meat conservative.'

ryan-chamberlin@FL.notes_set: Hardline-conservative record, well-cited. Life: YEA on HB289 (2026) fetal personhood; FRTL-endorsed 2024. Marriage/conscience: sponsored HB599 (2023) barring state pronoun mandates and protecting biology-based/religious-conviction belief. Public justice: prime sponsor CS/CS/HB113 (2025, flee-LEO penalties, enacted); co-sponsor HB383 LEO firearms. Immigration: YEA on SB2-C (2025) enforcement package. 2A: co-sponsor HB759 (lower long-gun age, repeal post-Parkland restriction) + HB6025 (repeal emergency firearm-seizure). Property-tax-relief sponsor. Prior 97/100 page cells on Family-Sovereignty/Election-Integrity/CBDC were uncited — nulled here pending evidence. No cross-party deviations.

0.note: Voted YEA on HB289 (2026) fetal-personhood bill; endorsed by Florida Right to Life 2024 — affirms personhood from conception.

2.note: Sponsored HB599 (2023) protecting biology-based belief and barring state pronoun mandates — affirms immutable biological sex.

4.note: HB599 restricts state-funded SOGI/gender-identity training and considerations — opposes LGBTQ promotion in public policy.

1.note: HB599 (2023) shields employees from adverse action for deeply-held religious beliefs — conscience exemptions.

2.note: HB599 bars compelled use of preferred pronouns on religious-conviction grounds — opposes compelled speech.

0.note: Prime sponsor of CS/CS/HB113 (2025, fleeing/eluding LEO penalties, enacted Ch. 2025-75) and co-sponsor HB383 — affirmative pro-law-enforcement record.

2.note: Voted YEA on SB2-C (2025 special session) DeSantis immigration-enforcement package (85-30).

1.note: Co-sponsor HB759 (2025) repealing post-Parkland long-gun age-21 restriction and HB6025 repealing emergency firearm-seizure authority — opposes gun restrictions.

profile.background: D, FL Senate SD-25 (Osceola + S. Orange). Seated Nov 5 2024; term to Nov 2028. Prior FL House HD-43/HD-46 (2020-24). James Madison Inst. FreedomIndex lifetime score 8%.

kristen-aston-arrington@FL.notes_set: Uniformly progressive record, heavily cited. Verified NAY votes: SB300 (6-week abortion ban), HB1223 (lower gun age), SB4 (2025 immigration enforcement), HB601 (banning civilian police-review boards), SB1804 (death penalty for child-sex traffickers). Endorsed by Planned Parenthood PAC, Equality Florida, Rainbow Democrats, Giffords PAC, Moms Demand Action, teachers' union. Opposed HB1557 (filed hostile amendment) and HB1 universal vouchers. Public-justice signal is mixed — PBA endorsement but NAY on the pro-police HB601, so q0 left null. No conservative cross-party deviations. NOTE: FreedomIndex legislator-summary Yea/Nay column rendered inverted; all hard votes verified against primary roll calls.

0.note: Voted NO on SB300 (6-week Heartbeat Protection Act); endorsed by Florida Planned Parenthood PAC — pro-choice, contrary to personhood-from-conception.

1.note: Voted NO on SB300 abortion restriction — opposes restricting abortion.

4.note: Accepted Florida Planned Parenthood PAC endorsement (abortion-industry support).

0.note: Endorsed by Equality Florida Action PAC + Rainbow Democrats; filed hostile amendment to HB1557 — does not affirm man-woman-only marriage.

4.note: Equality Florida / Rainbow Democrats endorsement; opposed HB1557 parental-rights-in-education — supports LGBTQ promotion in public policy/schools.

0.note: Endorsed by Orange County Classroom Teachers Association; voted against HB1 universal ESA vouchers — opposes school choice.

2.note: Voted NAY on SB1804 (2025) death penalty for child-sex traffickers — contrary to proportional punishment of evildoers.
Voted NO on SB4 (2025) 'Punishing Illegal Aliens' state enforcement bill — opposes immigration enforcement/preemption.

0.note: Endorsed by Giffords PAC and Moms Demand Action; voted NO on HB1223 lowering gun-purchase age — opposes expanded gun rights/constitutional carry.

1.note: Endorsed by gun-control orgs (Giffords, Moms Demand Action) — supports gun restrictions.

profile.background: R, FL HD-25 (Lake County). STATUS CORRECTION: second-term incumbent — first elected Nov 2022, re-elected Nov 2024 (64.95%); term to Nov 2026 (NOT a freshman / NOT a 2024 special-election winner). Business owner/contractor; licensed NRA firearms instructor; Chair, Lake County REC.

taylor-yarkosky@FL.notes_set: Strongly and consistently conservative, evidence-rich. Confirmed YEA votes: SB300 (6-week abortion ban), HB1069 (Parental Rights in Education expansion / binary sex), HB1 (universal school choice), SB7054 (CBDC ban), SB1718 (E-Verify immigration enforcement), SB4-C (2025 criminalize illegal entry), HB543 (permitless carry), HB1205 (initiative-petition tightening). Co-sponsor HB1119 (2026 materials harmful to minors) and HB759 (lower long-gun age). NRA member / firearms instructor. No cross-party deviations. Status corrected: second-term incumbent, not freshman. Uncited cells (religious liberty, faith-based adoption, anti-ESG, SB254) left null.

0.note: Voted YEA on SB300 (2023) 6-week Heartbeat Protection Act — documented pro-life vote.

2.note: Voted YEA on HB1069 (2023) defining sex as binary, restricting gender/pronoun instruction — affirms biological sex.

4.note: HB1069 restricts LGBTQ/sex instruction in K-12 — opposes LGBTQ promotion in schools.

0.note: Voted YEA on HB1 (2023) universal school-choice / Family Empowerment Scholarship expansion.

2.note: HB1069 restricts gender-ideology/sex instruction in K-12.

3.note: Co-sponsor HB1119 (2026) on materials harmful to minors / parental-objection process.

0.note: Voted YEA on SB7054 (2023) excluding central-bank digital currency from 'money' — opposes CBDC.

2.note: Voted YEA on SB4-C (2025) criminalizing illegal entry/reentry into FL — supports state immigration enforcement/preemption.

3.note: Voted YEA on SB1718 (2023) mandating E-Verify for employers 25+.

0.note: Voted YEA on HB543 (2023) permitless/constitutional carry.

1.note: Co-sponsor HB759 (2025) lowering long-gun purchase age, repealing post-Parkland restriction — opposes gun restrictions.
